Since `modules/common/system.nix` is imported by all hosts, defining `operation = "boot";` as a direct assignment prevents individual hosts (such as headless servers or non-NVIDIA machines where live switching is preferred) from overriding this setting without resorting to `lib.mkForce`.

Using `lib.mkDefault "boot"` allows individual host configurations to easily override the upgrade operation back to `"switch"` using a standard assignment, keeping the configuration flexible and consistent with other default settings in this file.

```
      operation = lib.mkDefault "boot";
```

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. In NixOS, avoid multiple lib.mkDefault definitions for the same option to prevent evaluation collisions. Use a direct assignment to enforce a value and override other defaults.
</details>","created_at":"2026-06-16T13:04:09Z","updated_at":"2026-06-16T13:04:09Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/571#discussion_r3420912646","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/571","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3420912646"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/571#discussion_r3420912646"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/571"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3420912646/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":null,"original_start_line":null,"start_side":null,"line":80,"original_line":80,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":7,"position":7,"subject_type":"line"}]

content.text: The suggestion is reasonable — using `lib.mkDefault` lets individual hosts override the operation without `lib.mkForce`. Let me verify `lib` is in scope.

input.command: head -5 modules/common/system.nix && echo "---mkDefault usage---" && grep -n "mkDefault" modules/common/system.nix | head
input.description: Check lib scope and mkDefault usage

content.text: `lib` is in scope and `mkDefault` is the established pattern in this file. Applying the change.

input.command: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/571/comments/3420912646/replies -f body='Done — changed to `operation = lib.mkDefault "boot";` so individual hosts (e.g. headless/non-NVIDIA) can override back to `"switch"` with a plain assignment, matching the other mkDefault settings in this file.'

content.text:
Done. There was one review comment from the Gemini code-assist bot.

**What I changed:** In `modules/common/system.nix`, changed `operation = "boot";` to `operation = lib.mkDefault "boot";`. Since this module is imported by all hosts, the plain assignment would have forced every host to use `"boot"` and required `lib.mkForce` to override. Using `lib.mkDefault` lets individual hosts (headless servers, non-NVIDIA machines) override back to `"switch"` with a standard assignment, consistent with the other `mkDefault` settings already in this file.

I parse-checked the file, committed (`948c0a7`), pushed — pre-commit hooks (nixfmt, detect-private-keys) passed — and replied to the review comment.
